name: Grilled Vegetable Fajitas
recipeIngredient: 1 Medium Eggplant, cut lengthwise into 1/4 inch slices
1 Medium Zucchini, cut lengthwise into 1/4 inch slices
1 Medium Sweet Red Peppers, cut into wedges
2 Cups Shredded Monterey Jack Cheese with Hot Pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly brush the rack of an outdoor grill or broiler pan with oil.
2. Preheat the grill or broiler.
3. Place batches of vegetables on the rack, about 3 to 4 inches from the heat.
4. Cook until nearly crisp-tender, about 3 minutes. Turn and brush with Southwestern Butter Baste.
5. Cook until crisp-tender, about 3 more minutes; remove and place on a large cutting board. Repeat with remaining vegetables.
6. Slice the vegetables into bite-size pieces and place in a serving bowl.
7. Drizzle with any remaining Southwestern Butter Baste and toss to coat.
8. For each serving: Heat flour tortillas according to package directions.
9. Place two tortillas on an individual serving plate. Top each with one lettuce leaf.
10. Place 3/4 cup vegetables and 1/4 cup Monterey Jack cheese down the center of each tortilla.
11. Fold the tortilla toward the center. Repeat.
Southwestern Butter Baste: In a small saucepan, melt 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) butter. Add 2 tablespoons chopped cilantro, 1 teaspoon chili powder, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon ground red pepper. Cook and stir over low heat for about 2 minutes to blend flavors. Stir in 2 tablespoons lime juice; remove from heat. Makes 1/3 cup.
For Grilled Italian Vegetable Fajitas, use 1 3/4 cups shredded mozzarella cheese and 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese in place of the Monterey Jack cheese.
Scampi Butter: In a small saucepan, melt 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) butter. Add 1/4 cup chopped fresh basil or parsley, 1 teaspoon minced garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/8 teaspoon black pepper. Cook and stir over low heat for 2 minutes to blend flavors. Stir in 2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar; remove from heat. Makes 1/2 cup.
